US: US1: Save and quickly insert predefined responses in chats
AC: AC1: Add Saved Response button in Editor nav
Step:
1. Login as Coach
2. Navigate to a conversation
3. Observe the Editor nav
4. Verify Saved Responses button
Title: Saved Responses button present next to GIF
Expectation: - SHOULD display a Saved Responses button positioned next to the GIF button in the Editor nav

Step:
1. Hover over the Saved Responses button
2. Verify tooltip
Title: Saved Responses button tooltip on hover
Expectation: - SHOULD show tooltip with text "Saved Responses"

Step:
1. Login as Coach
2. Navigate to a conversation
3. Click the Saved Responses button
4. Verify modal
Title: Saved Responses modal opens on button click
Expectation: - SHOULD display the Saved Responses modal

Step:
1. Press Ctrl/Command+K shortcut
2. Verify modal
Title: Saved Responses modal opens via keyboard shortcut
Expectation: - SHOULD display the Saved Responses modal

Title: Modal contains X button, Search box, Create new button
Expectation:
- SHOULD display X button
- SHOULD display Search box
- SHOULD display + Create New button in top-right corner

Step:
1. Hover over + Create New button
2. Verify button label
Title: Create New button hover label
Expectation: - SHOULD display as "+ Create New" on hover

Step:
1. Open modal with no saved responses
2. Verify empty state
Title: Empty state display when no saved responses
Expectation:
- SHOULD display an image
- SHOULD display text: "Your saved responses list is empty. Start by creating your first one to save time during chats."

Step:
1. Open modal with saved responses present
2. Verify list layout
Title: Left panel shows list of saved response titles
Expectation: - SHOULD display list of saved response titles on left side of modal

Title: List ordered by latest updated time
Expectation: - SHOULD order saved responses by latest updated time

Title: FREQUENTLY USED section shows top 5 most-used responses
Expectation:
- SHOULD display FREQUENTLY USED section with top 5 responses sorted by usage_count descending
- SHOULD sort by title A-Z when usage_count is equal

Title: FREQUENTLY USED section shows fewer than 5 when available
Expectation: - SHOULD display all available responses in FREQUENTLY USED section when fewer than 5 exist

Title: FREQUENTLY USED section hidden when no frequently used responses
Expectation: - SHOULD hide the FREQUENTLY USED section when no responses have been used

Title: ALL section lists all saved responses A-Z
Expectation: - SHOULD display ALL section with all saved responses sorted by title A-Z

Title: First saved response selected by default
Expectation: - SHOULD select the first saved response in the list by default

Step:
1. Press Up/Down arrow keys in modal
2. Verify selection movement
Title: Arrow keys navigate between saved responses
Expectation: - SHOULD move selection up and down through the list using Up/Down arrow keys

Step:
1. Select a saved response in the list
2. Verify right panel
Title: Right panel shows title and message detail of selected response
Expectation: - SHOULD display the title and message detail of the currently selected saved response on the right side

Step:
1. Hover over message detail on right panel
2. Verify 3-dot button
Title: 3-dot button appears on message detail hover
Expectation: - SHOULD show 3-dot button on hover over the message detail

Title: Message detail scrollable when long
Expectation: - SHOULD allow scrolling when message content is long

Step:
1. Hover over a saved response title in the list
2. Verify right panel updates
Title: Hovering title previews message detail on right panel
Expectation: - SHOULD update right panel to show message detail of the hovered saved response

Step:
1. Scroll down in modal with more than 20 responses
2. Verify pagination
Title: Pagination loads 20 items initially and on scroll
Expectation:
- SHOULD load 20 items per page
- SHOULD display Load more and fetch next 20 items when user scrolls down

Step:
1. Observe modal footer
2. Verify shortcut hints
Title: Footer shows keyboard shortcut hints
Expectation: - SHOULD display footer with: "↑↓ to navigate", "↵ to select", "Esc to close"

Step:
1. Click X button or press Esc
2. Verify modal
Title: X button / Esc closes modal
Expectation: - SHOULD close the Saved Responses modal

Step:
1. Open Saved Responses modal
2. Observe search box
Title: Search box default state
Expectation: - SHOULD display search box empty with placeholder "Search saved response"

Step:
1. Click/focus on search box
2. Verify placeholder
Title: Search box placeholder changes on focus
Expectation: - SHOULD change placeholder to "Enter keyword..." when focused

Step:
1. Type text in search box
2. Verify X icon
Title: X icon appears when text is entered
Expectation: - SHOULD display X icon when search input contains text

Step:
1. Type text in search box
2. Clear via backspace or click X icon
3. Verify list
Title: Clearing search input restores full list
Expectation: - SHOULD remove search input and display the full saved responses list when cleared

Step:
1. Type 1 character in search box
2. Verify search triggers
Title: Search triggers from 1 character
Expectation: - SHOULD start searching from the first character entered

Step:
1. Enter a keyword matching title or message
2. Verify results
Title: Search matches title and message fields case-insensitively
Expectation: - SHOULD return all saved responses where title or message contains the keyword (case-insensitive, partial match)

Step:
1. Enter a keyword with no matches
2. Verify empty result state
Title: No results state on search
Expectation:
- SHOULD display an image
- SHOULD display text: "Search results (0)"

Step:
1. Enter a keyword with matching results
2. Verify result count label
Title: Search result count displayed below search box
Expectation: - SHOULD display "Search results ({number_of_matched_results})" below the search box

Title: Search results show list with first item selected
Expectation:
- SHOULD display matched results in the same list format as AC2
- SHOULD select the first result by default

Step:
1. Search with more than 20 matching results
2. Scroll down
3. Verify pagination
Title: Search results pagination loads 20 at a time
Expectation:
- SHOULD load 20 items initially
- SHOULD display Load more and fetch next 20 items when user scrolls down

Step:
1. Open Saved Responses modal
2. Hover over a saved response message detail
3. Click the 3-dot button
4. Verify submenu
Title: 3-dot submenu shows Edit and Delete options
Expectation: - SHOULD display submenu with options: Edit response, Delete response

AC: AC5: Add a new saved response
Step:
1. Open Saved Responses modal
2. Click + Create New button
3. Verify Create Saved Response popup
Title: Create Saved Response popup opens
Expectation:
- SHOULD display popup with title "Create Saved Response"
- SHOULD display [X] button, TITLE field, MESSAGE field, Cancel button, Save button

Step:
1. Click [X] button in Create Saved Response popup
2. Verify popup
Title: X button closes Create Saved Response popup
Expectation: - SHOULD close the Create Saved Response popup

Step:
1. Observe TITLE field
2. Verify info icon tooltip
Title: TITLE field info icon tooltip
Expectation:
- SHOULD display (info) icon next to TITLE field label
- SHOULD show tooltip: "Used to organize responses. This won't appear in messages.." on hover

Step:
1. Observe TITLE field default state
2. Verify placeholder
Title: TITLE field empty by default with placeholder
Expectation: - SHOULD be empty with placeholder: "Name this response for easy search"

Step:
1. Type 140 characters in TITLE field
2. Attempt to type more
3. Verify input
Title: TITLE field max 140 character limit
Expectation: - SHOULD not allow input beyond 140 characters

Step:
1. Paste text longer than 140 characters in TITLE field
2. Verify result
Title: TITLE field paste truncated to 140 characters
Expectation: - SHOULD paste only the first 140 characters when pasted text exceeds the limit

Step:
1. Observe MESSAGE field default state
2. Verify placeholder
Title: MESSAGE field empty by default with placeholder
Expectation: - SHOULD be empty with placeholder: "Write the full message you want to send to clients"

Step:
1. Type in MESSAGE field
2. Verify character count display
Title: MESSAGE field character count shown
Expectation: - SHOULD display character count as "{X}/10000"

Step:
1. Type 10000 characters in MESSAGE field
2. Attempt to type more
3. Verify input
Title: MESSAGE field max 10,000 character limit
Expectation: - SHOULD not allow input beyond 10,000 characters

Step:
1. Paste text longer than 10,000 characters in MESSAGE field
2. Verify result
Title: MESSAGE field paste truncated to 10,000 characters
Expectation: - SHOULD paste only the first 10,000 characters when pasted text exceeds the limit

Step:
1. Paste formatted text (bold, italic, etc.) into MESSAGE field
2. Verify formatting
Title: MESSAGE field strips text formatting on paste
Expectation: - SHOULD clear any text formatting from pasted content

Step:
1. Observe Cancel button state
2. Verify
Title: Cancel button always enabled
Expectation: - SHOULD always be enabled regardless of field state

Step:
1. Click Cancel button
2. Verify popup
Title: Cancel button closes Create Saved Response popup
Expectation: - SHOULD close the Create Saved Response popup

Step:
1. Leave TITLE or MESSAGE field empty
2. Observe Save button
Title: Save button disabled when fields are empty
Expectation: - SHOULD be disabled when either TITLE or MESSAGE field has no input

Step:
1. Fill in both TITLE and MESSAGE fields
2. Observe Save button
Title: Save button enabled with both fields filled
Expectation: - SHOULD be enabled when both TITLE and MESSAGE fields have input

Step:
1. Fill in both fields
2. Click Save button
3. Verify result
Title: Save button creates new saved response and shows toast
Expectation:
- SHOULD add the new saved response to the list
- SHOULD display toast: "New Saved Response created."

Step:
1. Open Saved Responses modal
2. Hover over a saved response message detail
3. Click 3-dot button
4. Click Edit response
5. Verify Edit Saved Response popup
Title: Edit Saved Response popup opens
Expectation:
- SHOULD display popup with title "Edit Saved Response"
- SHOULD pre-populate TITLE and MESSAGE fields with existing values

Step:
1. Open Edit popup without making changes
2. Click [X] button
3. Verify
Title: X button closes Edit popup when no changes made
Expectation: - SHOULD close the Edit Saved Response popup

Step:
1. Make a change in Edit popup
2. Click [X] button
3. Verify Discard Changes popup
Title: X button triggers Discard Changes popup when changes exist
Expectation:
- SHOULD display Discard Changes popup with title "Discard Changes?"
- SHOULD display text: "Are you sure you want to go? Changes have not been saved yet."
- SHOULD display Cancel button and [X] icon and Discard Changes button

Step:
1. When Discard Changes popup is shown
2. Click Cancel or [X] in Discard Changes popup
3. Verify
Title: Cancel / X in Discard Changes popup closes the discard popup only
Expectation: - SHOULD close the Discard Changes popup and return to the Edit Saved Response popup

Step:
1. When Discard Changes popup is shown
2. Click Discard Changes button
3. Verify
Title: Discard Changes button closes edit popup without saving
Expectation: - SHOULD close Discard Changes popup and Edit Saved Response popup without saving changes

Step:
1. Open Edit popup without making changes
2. Click Cancel button
3. Verify
Title: Cancel button closes Edit popup when no changes made
Expectation: - SHOULD close the Edit Saved Response popup

Step:
1. Make a change in Edit popup
2. Click Cancel button
3. Verify Discard Changes popup
Title: Cancel button triggers Discard Changes popup when changes exist
Expectation: - SHOULD display Discard Changes popup (same as [X] button behavior)

Step:
1. Fill in both TITLE and MESSAGE fields in Edit popup
2. Click Save button
3. Verify result
Title: Save button updates response and shows toast
Expectation:
- SHOULD save the changes for that response
- SHOULD display toast: "Saved Response updated successfully."

Step:
1. Open Saved Responses modal
2. Hover over a saved response message detail
3. Click 3-dot button
4. Click Delete response
5. Verify confirmation popup
Title: Delete confirmation popup shown
Expectation:
- SHOULD display popup with title "Delete Response?" - SHOULD display text: "This response will be permanently deleted and can't be recovered.
Are you sure you want to proceed?" - SHOULD display Cancel button and OK button

Step:
1. Click Cancel in delete confirmation popup
2. Verify
Title: Cancel closes delete confirmation popup
Expectation: - SHOULD close the delete confirmation popup without deleting

Step:
1. Click OK in delete confirmation popup
2. Verify result
Title: OK deletes response and shows toast
Expectation: - SHOULD close the popup, delete the response, and display toast: "Saved Response deleted."

AC: AC8: Add saved response to chatbox
Step:
1. Open Saved Responses modal
2. Click title of a saved response or press Enter
3. Verify chatbox
Title: Selecting saved response appends message to chatbox
Expectation: - SHOULD append the full message content of the selected response into the chatbox behind existing text

US: US2: Create a saved response from a message in conversation
AC: AC1: Add Save this response button to Coach's non-text message
Step:
1. Login as Coach
2. Navigate to a conversation
3. Hover over a non-text message sent by Coach
4. Verify bookmark button
Title: Bookmark button appears on hover for Coach non-text messages
Expectation: - SHOULD display a bookmark button between the 3-dot and Reply buttons for Coach's non-text messages

Step:
1. Hover over the bookmark button
2. Verify tooltip
Title: Bookmark button tooltip on hover
Expectation: - SHOULD show tooltip: "Saved this response"

Step:
1. Login as Coach on a workspace NOT on Pro/Studio plan
2. Hover over a non-text Coach message
3. Verify bookmark button
Title: Bookmark button hidden on non-paid plan workspaces
Expectation: - SHOULD NOT display the bookmark button for workspaces not on Pro or Studio plan

AC: AC2: Add new saved response from conversation
Step:
1. Click the bookmark button on a Coach non-text message
2. Verify popup
Title: Add To Saved Responses popup opens with pre-populated message
Expectation:
- SHOULD display popup with title "Add To Saved Responses"
- SHOULD leave TITLE field blank
- SHOULD pre-populate MESSAGE field with the Coach's message content

Step:
1. Click bookmark button on a Coach message with over 10,000 characters
2. Verify MESSAGE field content
Title: MESSAGE pre-populated truncated to 10,000 characters
Expectation: - SHOULD pre-populate MESSAGE field with only the first 10,000 characters of the message

Step:
1. Fill in TITLE field
2. Click Save button
3. Verify result
Title: Save button adds new response and shows toast from conversation
Expectation:
- SHOULD add the message as a new saved response
- SHOULD display toast: "Response has been saved"
